title: Quick Reaction
description: Choose the best reaction for each surprise situation. Think fast!

questions.question: Your friend tells you they just got ENGAGED! You say:
options: Congratulations! I'm so happy for you!
questions.explanation: An engagement is exciting news! Show genuine enthusiasm and happiness.

questions.question: You accidentally BUMP INTO someone on the street. You say:
options: Oh, I'm so sorry! Are you okay?
questions.explanation: Apologize immediately and show concern. This is the polite and natural reaction.

questions.question: Someone gives you an UNEXPECTED GIFT. You say:
options: "You shouldn't have! Thank you so much!"
questions.explanation: "You shouldn't have!" is a warm, natural way to express surprise and gratitude.

questions.question: Someone sneezes. You say: "___ you!"
questions.explanation: "Bless you!" is the standard polite response when someone sneezes in English.

questions.question: A friend tells you they LOST THEIR JOB. You say:
options: "I'm really sorry to hear that. Is there anything I can do?"
questions.explanation: Show empathy and offer support. This is the caring and appropriate reaction.

questions.question: Your colleague suggests an idea you DISAGREE with. You say:
options: "I see your point, but I think we could also consider..."
questions.explanation: Diplomatic disagreement: acknowledge their point, then offer your alternative.

questions.question: Someone thanks you for helping. You say: "You're ___! / No ___!"
questions.explanation: "You're welcome!" or "No problem!" are both natural responses to thanks.

questions.question: You run into an OLD FRIEND you haven't seen in years. You say:
options: "Oh my gosh! It's been forever! How have you been?"
"I remember you but I don't want to talk."
questions.explanation: Show excitement and genuine interest. "It's been forever!" is a natural expression.

questions.question: Someone asks you a DIFFICULT QUESTION you need time to answer. You say:
options: "That's a great question. Let me think about that for a moment."
questions.explanation: Buy time gracefully: compliment the question, then take a moment to think.

questions.question: Your friend says they're NERVOUS about a presentation. You say:
options: "You'll do great! Just be yourself and speak clearly."
questions.explanation: Encourage and support. Reassurance with practical advice is the best approach.

title: Yes, And...
description: Construct responses that accept and expand the interlocutor's idea.

sentences.prompt: Friend says: "I just booked a trip to Japan!"
sentences.correct: That's amazing! We should try authentic sushi!

sentences.prompt: Colleague says: "We should organize a team dinner."
sentences.correct: Great idea! And we could try that new Italian place.

sentences.prompt: Friend says: "I'm thinking about learning guitar."
sentences.correct: That's awesome! We could take lessons together!

sentences.prompt: Someone says: "The weather is beautiful today!"
sentences.correct: You're right! We should go for a walk in the park.

sentences.prompt: Friend says: "I just got a promotion at work!"
sentences.correct: Congratulations! You totally deserve it! We should celebrate!

sentences.prompt: Someone says: "This movie looks really interesting."
sentences.correct: Definitely! Let's watch it this weekend.

title: The Roulette of Situations
description: Spin the wheel and react to the situation you face. Practice your reflexes!

title: Reacciones Naturales
description: Build your deck of spontaneous reactions for any situation.

cards.back: It just can't be! / No way! — Informal, high level of surprise. Variants: "You're kidding!", "Are you serious?"
cards.example: You got the job? No way! That's incredible!

cards.back: I'm very happy for you! — Shows genuine joy at another's success.
cards.example: You're getting married? I'm so happy for you!

cards.back: I'm sorry to hear that. — Show empathy in the face of bad news.
cards.example: You lost your job? I'm sorry to hear that. How can I help?

cards.back: You shouldn't have (bothered)! — Reaction to an unexpected gift. Positive and grateful.
cards.example: You got me flowers? You shouldn't have! Thank you so much!

cards.back: Health! — It is said automatically when someone sneezes. Cultural.

cards.front: I SEE YOUR POINT, BUT... 🤔 (Diplomatic disagreement)
cards.back: I understand your point, but... — Polite disagreement that validates the other first.
cards.example: I see your point, but I think we should consider other options.

cards.back: Good question. — Gain time to think. Professional and natural.
cards.example: That's a great question. Let me think about that for a moment.

cards.back: A century has passed! — For when you see someone after a long time.
cards.example: Oh my gosh! It's been forever! How have you been?

cards.back: What a relief! —When a concern is resolved positively.
cards.example: The exam was canceled? What a relief!

cards.back: By the way... / By the way... — Natural transition to another topic.
cards.example: By the way, have you heard about the new restaurant downtown?

cards.back: You will do great! — Expression of support and trust.
cards.example: Don't worry about the interview. You'll do great!

cards.back: How frustrating! — Show that you share the other's frustration.
cards.example: Your flight was delayed again? How frustrating!

title: Situation → Reaction
description: Match each situation with the most appropriate verbal reaction.

title: Quick Reaction Listening
description: Listen and choose the correct answer.

That's awesome! We could take lessons together
You're right! We should go for a walk in the park
